detailed_analysis: opencode_io.py is a Layer-3 interface adapter implementing the CustomizationTypeIO contract (parse/render) for opencode `agent` .md and `SKILL.md` documents. It is clean, cohesive, and well-decomposed: all five private/public helpers and the four parse/render entry points stay under the 40-line function limit, names follow the two-word/role-domain convention, there are no hardcoded secrets, no injection vectors, no global mutable state, no bare-except swallowing, and no mutable default arguments. The module correctly delegates name-resolution policy to a shared helper (resolve_artifact_name) and metadata-block handling to markdown_yaml_metadata_block, keeping the adapter thin. The single structural smell is module length: at 319 lines the file exceeds the project's 300-line module guideline (NFR-14 / AGENTS.md §3). The dominant maintainability concern is heavy duplication between the agent and skill parse/render pairs and a clutch of module-level frozenset/tuple field-list constants that read as primitive-obsession passthrough configuration. No secret-bearing fields (env/headers/auth) are rendered here — those belong to the mcp_server factory — so NFR-15 egress enforcement is out of scope for this adapter and correctly absent. The adapter satisfies the Dependency Rule: it imports only inward (canonical, artifact_names, markdown_yaml_metadata_block) and references the concrete tool name only as the literal string key "opencode" inside its own per-tool bags, which is permitted for adapter modules per architecture §1. Round-trip stability (US-10 AC-1, NFR-06) holds for the model provider split/join and the metadata passthrough; the skill renderer re-slugs `name` via opencode_skill_slug while the parser reads `name` verbatim, which is the declared slugify_name behaviour rather than a defect.

dependency_graph_summary.dependency_rule: respected: imports point inward only; no use-case or framework imports; concrete tool name appears only as the adapter's own bag key "opencode"

findings.evidence: File spans 319 physical lines (module ends at __all__, line 309-318).
findings.problem: Module is 319 lines, exceeding the project's explicit 300-line file/module limit (AGENTS.md §3, NFR-14). It is not a God Module — cohesion is high and responsibilities are narrow (parse/render of two opencode customization_types) — but the field-list constants (lines 26-96) and the near-duplicate agent/skill pairs push it over the bar. Derogation is plausible but undocumented.

findings.evidence:
per_only = dict(canonical.get("per_agentic_tool_only") or {})
per_only["opencode"] = opencode_only
canonical["per_agentic_tool_only"] = per_only
per_extra = dict(canonical.get("per_agentic_tool_extra") or {})
per_extra["opencode"] = unknown_metadata_fields(...)
canonical["per_agentic_tool_extra"] = per_extra
if "pair_id" in frontmatter_data: ... elif prior_canonical is None: canonical["pair_id"] = new_pair_id()
findings.problem: The per_agentic_tool_only / per_agentic_tool_extra bag wiring and the pair_id fallback block are repeated verbatim between parse_opencode_agent_md (190-205) and parse_opencode_skill_md (262-277). The same shape recurs in the two render functions' trailing extra-field loops (236-238, 302-304). Three-plus repetitions cross the Rule-of-Three threshold (AGENTS.md §3) and invite drift between the agent and skill code paths.

findings.evidence:
def parse_opencode_agent_md(text, prior_canonical=None, *, artifact_path=None):
    ... name resolution, description, metadata_subset, maxSteps/permission/tools deprecation handling, model split, two bag writes, pair_id fallback ...
findings.problem: parse_opencode_agent_md packs several distinct concerns — name precedence resolution, deprecated `tools`->`permission` migration (179-182), maxSteps->steps aliasing (177-178), model/provider splitting, and bag assembly. Body code (excluding the 6-line docstring) is right at the 40-line ceiling with ~8 branch points. Still readable, but the deprecated-field migration logic is a candidate to extract into a named helper alongside _normalise_deprecated_tools.

The slug truncation length 64 is an inline magic number with no named constant or comment stating it is opencode's skill-name length cap (AGENTS.md §9: name magic numbers as constants with unit and intent). The fallback string "converted" is repeated at lines 137 and 138; a named constant would document intent and avoid drift.

notes: US-10 AC-1/AC-4: adapter exposes pure parse/render and writes per_agentic_tool_only["opencode"] and per_agentic_tool_extra["opencode"] verbatim (lines 190-205, 262-277) — conforms.
Protocol §parse purity / unknown-field passthrough: unknown fields preserved via unknown_metadata_fields (195-199, 267-271) — conforms (US-10 AC; protocol line 152).
extract_pair_id never raises: adapter re-exports markdown_yaml_metadata_block.extract_pair_id_from_md (line 312); the helper handles malformed input — contract satisfied (protocol line 148).
NFR-06 round-trip stability: model provider split (108-114) and join (117-124) are inverse over the supported cases; metadata passthrough is verbatim.

NFR-15 / goal 6 secret policy: opencode_io renders only agent/skill frontmatter (no env/headers/auth fields); secret-policy egress enforcement belongs to the mcp_server factory and sync core, correctly absent here.

architecture §1 'sync engine never references a concrete tool name': the literal "opencode" appears only as this adapter's own per-tool bag key and frontmatter selector — permitted for adapter modules, not a use-case leak.
Dependency Rule (§3): imports are inward only (artifact_names, canonical, markdown_yaml_metadata_block); no Layer-2/Layer-4 imports.

verdict_summary: Clean, contract-conformant Layer-3 opencode adapter; only minor structure/maintainability smells (319-line module over the 300 limit, agent/skill duplication, a magic 64). No CRITICAL or MAJOR findings; code_score 0.75.
